what_it_does: Embeds 29 operator role definitions in two ways through a local :8007 /v1/embeddings endpoint and compares the resulting vectors: PLAIN embeds each role alone, CONTEXT embeds all roles as one parent document using late chunking, then it measures how much vectors shift and whether operator-family separability changes.
observations: Uses AST parsing to extract the ROLE dictionary from each .py file in /home/tim/company/roles and builds a text string from id, label, description, trigger, output, and context fields.
Calls the same endpoint twice with different request shapes: {input:texts} for plain embeddings and {documents:[texts]} for context/late-chunking embeddings.
Quantizes embeddings to int8 in both requests.
Clusters the plain embeddings with scale.agglomerate and scale.cut into 8 groups, then applies that partition to both plain and context vectors.
Measures per-role cosine between plain and context vectors, intra/inter family cosine margins, and whether each role moves closer to its own family centroid under context.
Hardcodes paths, URL, model name, and a 300-second timeout.
standouts: Treats a set of code role definitions as a single parent document to test context-as-parent for non-document entities.
Quantifies the hypothesis with three concrete metrics: vector shift, family separability margin, and per-role centroid pull.
Requires no server changes or restarts, using an existing contextual endpoint.
conventions: Starts with a shebang and a detailed module docstring.
Inserts /home/tim/company into sys.path to import runtime.scale.
Uses urllib.request for HTTP and ast for source parsing.
Names local helpers plainly: role_text, post, cos, margin, centroid.
concerns: Hardcoded local paths and endpoint URL make the script environment-specific.
No error handling for network failures, malformed responses, or missing ROLE assignments.
Cluster count is fixed at 8 without justification in the file.
Uses int8 quantization, which may reduce embedding precision.
Judges context embeddings partly against centroids computed from plain embeddings, which may not reflect context-derived structure.
notes: A self-contained experimental script that validates whether embedding operator roles inside their full registry context produces more coherent family structure than embedding them alone.
questions: What does the scale module provide and where is it defined?
What are the intended operator families, and why is 8 the chosen number of clusters?
Does the endpoint at :8007 always return meta.document and meta.chunk fields?
Are all 29 role files guaranteed to contain a top-level ROLE assignment with an id key?
How were the endpoint model and int8 quantization selected?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it embeds roles both in isolation and as a parent group, then compares separability metrics to test the context-as-parent upgrade.
intention_signals: It is trying to provide empirical evidence that embedding an item together with its sibling/parent registry improves the geometric organization of operator families compared to embedding each item in isolation.
novelty: Repurposes a document late-chunking embedding endpoint to treat code role definitions as a single parent context, testing whether context-as-parent works for structured non-document objects.
contribution: Provides a reproducible comparison harness that loads role definitions, requests plain and contextual embeddings from the same endpoint, and reports shift and separability metrics for downstream evaluation.
summary_for_embedding: A Python proof script that embeds operator role definitions in isolation and as a single parent-context document, then compares vector shifts and family separability using cosine metrics and agglomerative clustering.
intention_for_embedding: To empirically test whether embedding operator roles within their full registry context improves operator-family coherence compared to plain per-role embeddings.

symbols.does: Parses a role Python file and extracts an id plus a concatenated text string from the ROLE dictionary.
symbols.description: Uses ast to find the top-level assignment to ROLE, reads keys such as id, label, description, trigger, output, and context, and returns a tuple of (id, text) suitable for embedding.

symbols.does: Sends a JSON POST to the local /v1/embeddings endpoint and returns the parsed response.
symbols.description: Wraps urllib.request to call the hardcoded URL with the provided request body, uses a 300-second timeout, and returns the response as parsed JSON.

symbols.does: Computes cosine similarity between two numeric vectors.
symbols.description: Calculates the dot product divided by the product of L2 norms, with a guard against zero norms.

symbols.does: Computes average intra-group, inter-group cosine similarity and their difference for a partition.
symbols.description: Iterates over the supplied groups, accumulates pairwise cosine similarities within each group and across groups, then returns the mean intra similarity, mean inter similarity, and the margin between them.

symbols.does: Computes the arithmetic mean vector for a list of vector indices.
symbols.description: Averages each dimension over the selected embedding vectors, producing a centroid used to measure whether a role moves toward its family center.
